title: Fighting reviewer fatigue or amplifying bias? Considerations and recommendations for use of ChatGPT and other large language models in scholarly peer review
key_finding: LLMs can facilitate higher quality reviews but raise concerns about bias, confidentiality, and reproducibility
evidence_quote: LLMs have the potential to substantially alter the role of both peer reviewers and editors. Through supporting both actors in efficiently writing constructive reports or decision letters, LLMs can facilitate higher quality review and address issues of review shortage. However, the fundamental opacity of LLMs' training data, inner workings, data handling, and development processes raise concerns about potential biases, confidentiality and the reproducibility of review reports.

title: Large language models for automated scholarly paper review: A survey
authors: Zhuang Z, Chen J, Xu H, et al.
key_finding: Comprehensive survey of LLMs in ASPR documenting methods, datasets, and systems
evidence_quote: LLMs hold transformative potential for the full-scale implementation of automated scholarly paper review (ASPR), but they also pose new issues and challenges that need to be addressed. In this survey paper, we aim to provide a holistic view of ASPR in the era of LLMs.

title: FLAWS: A Benchmark for Error Identification and Localization in Scientific Papers
authors: Xi S, Rao V, Payan J, et al.
key_finding: GPT 5 achieved 39.1% accuracy in identifying claim-invalidating errors in scientific papers
evidence_quote: On the resulting benchmark, we evaluate five frontier LLMs: Claude Sonnet 4.5, DeepSeek Reasoner v3.1, Gemini 2.5 Pro, GPT 5, and Grok 4. Among these, GPT 5 is the top-performing model, achieving 39.1% identification accuracy when k=10, where k is the number of top-ranked error text candidates generated by the LLM.

title: Automated Research Review Support Using Machine Learning, Large Language Models, and Natural Language Processing
authors: Pendyala VS, Kamdar K, Mulchandani K
key_finding: System achieved 86% test accuracy on acceptance prediction using DistilBERT
evidence_quote: The experiments result in a 86% test accuracy on acceptance prediction using DistilBERT. Results from other models are comparable, with PaLM-based LLM embeddings achieving 84% accuracy.

title: Large language models in peer review: challenges and opportunities
key_finding: Critical perspective on LLM challenges and opportunities in peer review

title: Aspect-Guided Multi-Level Perturbation Analysis of Large Language Models in Automated Peer Review
authors: Li J, Li Y, Hu X, et al.
key_finding: LLM-based review systems show significant vulnerabilities to perturbations
evidence_quote: We identify several potential vulnerabilities: review conclusions that recommend a strong reject may significantly influence meta-reviews, negative or misleading reviews may be wrongly interpreted as thorough, and incomplete or hostile rebuttals can unexpectedly lead to higher acceptance rates. Statistical tests show that these biases persist under various Chain-of-Thought prompting strategies, highlighting the lack of robust critical evaluation in current LLMs.

title: ReviewGuard: Enhancing Deficient Peer Review Detection via LLM-Driven Data Augmentation
authors: Zhang H, Li R, Shrestha S, et al.
journal: ACM/IEEE Joint Conference on Digital Libraries
key_finding: LLM-driven system detects deficient reviews with F1 of 0.7073 using mixed training data
evidence_quote: Mixed training with synthetic and real data substantially improves detection performance—for example, Qwen 3-8B achieves recall of 0.6653 and F1 of 0.7073, up from 0.5499 and 0.5606 respectively.

title: Toward autonomous discovery: agentic AI and the future of ophthalmic research
authors: Soetikno BT, Nielsen CS, Pollreisz A, et al.
key_finding: Agentic AI systems can autonomously perform complex scientific tasks including peer review
evidence_quote: Recent studies demonstrate that agentic AI systems can autonomously execute tasks traditionally performed by human researchers, including peer review, hypothesis generation, systematic reviews, and experimental design.
